description_long: This object is a simple, stylized paper airplane with sharply creased wings and a pointed nose. The wings are broad and flat, extending outward in a straight line from the central fold, giving the airplane a classic dart-like appearance. The body is formed by a single central fold, creating a triangular cross-section that tapers to a fine point at the front. The surface is smooth and unadorned, with a uniform light gray color throughout. There are no visible engines, landing gear, or cockpit features, emphasizing its minimalistic, folded-paper construction. The overall design is lightweight and aerodynamic, suitable for gliding short distances when thrown by hand.
description: A minimalist, light gray paper airplane with sharply creased wings and a pointed nose, featuring a smooth, unadorned surface and a classic dart-like shape.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose and symmetrical triangular wings extending outward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thin, flat body and the sharp taper of the nose.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the V-shaped tail formed by the folded wings and central crease.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the flat, streamlined body and pointed tip.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no dihedral or anhedral angle; no winglets or additional features.
attribute_tail_design: No distinct tail assembly; rear is formed by the converging folds of the wings, resembling a simple V-tail.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear; rests flat on its underside.
attribute_cockpit_placement: No visible cockpit; no enclosed or open cockpit area present.
